meaning: n.净化,清除,泻药v.(使)净化,清除,肃清,(使)通便
examples: He closed his eyes and lay still, trying to purge his mind of anxiety.
In the Blood Purge of 1934, a Nazi SS squad murdered Kurt von Schleicher in the doorway of his home.
Licorice and mung bean do detox well, especially the mung bean, which is used to purge toxins in liver.
News Corp preferred to negotiate undertakings to purge the harm to plurality perceived by Ofcom.
The purge in Buffalo continued, with the Sabres dealing captain Jason Pominville to Minnesota on Wednesday.
